rules.annualReviewRule: Must occur before anniversary date; schedule meeting 1–2 weeks prior to due date

ruleTable.requirement: Must be sent at least 10 calendar days before the meeting (legal NOM from Enrich — email/text alone is not sufficient)

Begins on the date signed consent is received; eligibility meeting must be held within 60 calendar days. Does not apply to reevaluations by law — reevals must be finalized by the next eligibility due date.
Finalize Consent → Evaluations → Eligibility → Consent for Services (initials) → Eligibility PWN → IEP in order so dates populate correctly on the student grid.
Must occur before anniversary date; schedule 1–2 weeks prior to avoid a lapsed IEP.
Required every 3 years (or sooner if needed); complete by eligibility due date.
Parent must be notified within 5 days of referral.
Minimum 2 intervention cycles (about 6 weeks each) before SPED referral when intervention has not already occurred; DAT notes 6 weeks if deficits lack intervention.
Primary service provider / team lead must be a SpEd teacher or SLP.
Minutes logged in Enrich within 48 hours of service.
Issued quarterly, aligned with report cards.
Parent can revoke consent; document via PWN. Services stop per IDEA timelines (tracked as 15 days in profile calculator).
Required within 10 school days of a discipline removal totaling more than 10 days.
Required for students graduating or aging out.
Non-biased assessments in primary language when possible.
Required for all changes to identification, evaluation, placement, or FAPE.
Complete transfer within 5 days of notice student has an IEP. If IEP not adopted, hold Review IEP within 30 calendar days; reevaluation within 60 calendar days when required.
Enrich draft documents must follow Convert (when needed), Validate, then Finalize. Do not skip validation; overdue finalize requires state delay codes.

paths: MTSS meeting → fill referral → discuss evaluation with parents → if evaluating, Move to Evaluation + separate DAT referral on Groups page
New evaluation / team decides DAT → Groups page DAT referral (sender receives IEIF) + Enrich referral
notes: DAT referral and Enrich referral are TWO SEPARATE steps.
Whoever sends the DAT referral receives the IEIF form and must share with teammates (including nurse).
Do not move or copy IEIF links — DAT loses access.
Ensure Drive links grant DAT view permission.
Intervention must be in place for academic and social/emotional needs; if none, plan ~6 weeks.
Nurse completes health history; nurse attends if health concerns.
Online IEP meetings: each person on own device (muted). If parents in person, one DAT member attends in person.

complianceTicker.text: IDEA / Colorado: 60-day initial evaluation window from signed consent
NOM: Send legal Enrich notice at least 10 calendar days before meeting (CCSD)
Annual IEP: Review before anniversary — schedule 1–2 weeks early
FERPA: Student records confidential — need-to-know basis only
HIPAA: Protected health info — secure storage required; no live Enrich sync from PRISM
Enrich: Convert → Validate → Finalize; service logs within 48 hours
CLD: Non-biased assessment in primary language when possible
DAT: Dual referral required (Groups DAT form + Enrich) — do not move IEIF links
PWN required for ALL changes to identification, evaluation, placement, or FAPE

operatingModes.companion: External IEP system (e.g. Enrich) is system of record; PRISM drafts/reminds/exports
operatingModes.standalone: PRISM runs full suite including finalize and district templates

adminAccess.note: District administration is permission-gated (Admin menu). Pilot unlock code is client-side only.
